page: Amazon Web Services m6i.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Intel Xeon 8375C processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 16 virtual CPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz and is supported by a 54 MB L3 cache. It includes 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The instance runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

This instance configuration does not include local NVMe SSD storage or integrated GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached volumes. Network connectivity is defined by a baseline bandwidth of 6 Gbps. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are bundled with this instance type.

Benchmark results place the m6i.4xlarge in the average performance tier. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1621.0 and a multi-core score of 9798.0, alongside a Passmark CPU score of 20934.59. Memory performance shows a read-only large block bandwidth of 97.93 GB/sec and a write large block bandwidth of 57.44 GB/sec. In application-specific tests, the instance processes 15.63 million Redis SET operations per second and delivers a static web serving throughput of 21.5 GB/sec. For lightweight machine learning tasks, it handles 135M model prompt processing at 1103.68 tokens per second, though performance drops to 32.62 tokens per second for a 7B model.

Given its balanced resource allocation and dedicated CPU provisioning, the m6i.4xlarge offers a stable, predictable cost-to-performance ratio for standard enterprise workloads. The absence of local storage and GPUs makes it less suitable for heavy data-mining or real-time graphics rendering. However, its balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio and solid multi-core performance make it well-suited for applications such as web serving, database operations, software compilation, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
description: The Amazon Web Services m6i.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 vCPUs and 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory, running on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with an x86_64 architecture, it operates at 3.5 GHz with 8 physical cores and hyper-threading. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates stable, mid-range performance, with a Passmark CPU score of 20934.59, a static web serving throughput of 21.5 GB/sec, and 15.63 million Redis SET operations per second. Its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it a reliable, cost-efficient option for standard enterprise workloads, including web serving, database operations, and software compilation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6i.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8375C processor, delivering balanced performance for standard enterprise workloads.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and DDR4 memory for reliable enterprise application hosting.
bullet_points: 16 vCPUs and 64.0 GB DDR4 memory providing a balanced 1:4 resource ratio
Powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz
Delivers 6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Achieves 15.63 million Redis SET operations per second
Suited for web serving, database operations, and software compilation
